agents_md_issues: AGENTS.md excerpt focuses on SDEC×2SCV×ACR process but does not encode the repo’s current operational SSOT targets (Vercel URL, Postgres persistence checks, meta_checker metrics) as concrete mandatory steps.
No explicit “spin/stagnation” detection + automatic pause criteria is present in AGENTS.md guidance, even though the system already tracks commit types and run statuses.
Instruction effectiveness feedback loop is not specified (how to measure, where to record, how to update policy weights), so learning is implicit rather than enforced.

improvements.action: Add spin/stagnation guardrails that automatically pause or re-route the planner when output becomes low-signal.
improvements.file: tools/meta_checker.py (or equivalent metrics runner) + planner entrypoint (DynamicPlanner)
improvements.detail: Implement substantive_ratio computation from last N commits (same formula as in this task). If <0.3 => set should_pause=true and force a recovery instruction (e.g., ship/deploy/bugfix). Persist the computed ratio and decision to .audit/health.json each run.

improvements.action: Make instruction effectiveness measurable and self-adjusting (closed-loop policy weighting).
improvements.file: .audit/instruction_effectiveness.json + planner policy config (e.g., hub/policy/policy.current.md or equivalent)
improvements.detail: On each run, log {instruction_id, run_id, commits, substantive_commits, status}. Compute commits-per-run and substantive-commits-per-run over a rolling window. Update weights using a bounded rule (e.g., +0.05/-0.05) with min/max caps, and write a human-readable diff summary to .audit/policy_weight_changes.log.

improvements.action: Bring PURPOSE.md from “production-ready claim” to “production-ready proof” with live links and verification commands.
improvements.detail: Add a small “Production Proof (SSOT)” section with: current Vercel URL, last successful deploy timestamp, last migration applied, and the exact commands/CI job name that verifies (1) DB write/read (2) auth flows (3) meta_checker metrics run. Require that updates are generated from CI artifacts rather than manual edits.

improvements.action: Codify AGENTS.md into an executable checklist so agents stop relying on prose interpretation.
improvements.detail: Add a mandatory ‘Before Change / After Change’ checklist referencing concrete scripts (tests, lint, production-readiness validation). Provide a single command (e.g., scripts/run_ci_local.sh) that runs the same gates as CI and emits a machine-readable report consumed by meta_checker.

improvements.action: Reduce docs/audit noise by batching and gating documentation-only commits.
improvements.file: commit policy doc (e.g., AGENTS.md or contributing guide) + CI check
improvements.detail: Add a CI rule: if a PR/run produces only docs/audit/chore changes for K consecutive runs, force a substantive task selection next (feat/fix/test). Alternatively, require docs(audit) to include a linked metric delta (JudgmentScore/test/coverage) to justify the commit.

purpose_driven_executor.reason: Strong success rate, but commit mix suggests it can drift into docs/audit updates; slightly down-weight to prioritize feature-bearing loops while keeping it available for roadmap alignment.

spec_driven_dev.reason: Highest commits-per-run (1.0) with consistently successful outcomes and a higher likelihood of substantive implementation; up-weight to maximize real product change when already production-ready.

reasoning: Substantive work is exactly at the HEALTHY threshold (0.50). The system is not spinning (runs succeed; meaningful feats exist), but it lacks explicit, automated guardrails and a measurable learning loop (instruction → substantive output → weight update). Converting PURPOSE/AGENTS from prose to executable, audited checks will increase durability and prevent regression into audit-only churn.
